page: Microsoft Azure DS3 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8171M processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU features a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512F, AES cryptography, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 14.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.5 GB per core, and includes 29 GB of local storage. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates that the DS3 sits primarily in the lower performance tier for compute-heavy tasks. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Similarly, large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations are categorized as weak. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in specific workloads. It achieves 5.9 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and shows average throughput in small-model LLM inference, processing 499.15 tokens per second for prompts and 111.31 tokens per second for text generation using a 135M parameter model. Passmark extended instruction tests also register in the middle 50% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the DS3 offers a balanced allocation of memory and compute for entry-level workloads, though its older Intel Xeon architecture limits its raw processing efficiency compared to newer hardware generations. The primary tradeoff is the lower sequential memory bandwidth and weak multi-threaded decompression speeds, which restrict its utility for data-intensive processing. This instance is suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, small Redis caching layers, and minor development or testing environments that do not require high-throughput compute.
description: Microsoft Azure DS3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 14.0 GB of RAM (3.5 GB per core) and 29 GB of local storage, managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the bottom 25% for raw compute and large-block memory bandwidth. However, it maintains average performance in Redis SET operations, reaching 5.9 million operations per second, and small-model LLM inference. The DS3 represents an entry-tier general-purpose option, presenting a tradeoff of lower compute speeds for basic resource availability. It is suited for lightweight web serving, small caching layers, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M cores, 14.0 GB of RAM, and 29 GB of local storage, suited for lightweight workloads and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS3 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 4 Intel Xeon cores, 14.0 GB RAM, and 29 GB local storage for basic workloads.
tagline: An entry-tier general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and balanced memory for lightweight utility workloads and caching.
